ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.

ess: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Siberian Yupik.
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.

inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.

ires: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ire
iris: noun. A plant of the genus Iris, common in the northern hemisphere, and generally having attractive blooms (See Iris (plant) on Wikipedia.
issei: noun. A member of the first generation of Japanese immigrants to North America, South America, or Australia.
its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.

nest: noun. A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.

nisei: noun. One whose parents were Japanese immigrants, especially to North or South America.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.

nitre: noun. UK standard spelling of niter.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
rent: noun. A payment made by a tenant at intervals in order to lease a property.

resist: verb. To attempt to counter the actions or effects of.

ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
rins: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rin
rinse: verb. To wash (something) quickly using water and no soap.
rise: verb. To move, or appear to move, physically upwards relative to the ground.
rises: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rise
sei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seri.
seisin: noun. An entitlement to a freehold estate with a right to immediate possession.
sen: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nanerige.
seniti: noun. A Tongan unit of currency equivalent to a hundredth of a pa'anga.
sent: verb. simple past and past participle of send
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
sinter: noun. An alluvial sediment deposited by a mineral spring.
sir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siri.
sire: noun. A lord, master, or other person in authority, most commonly used vocatively: formerly in speaking to elders and superiors, later only
siren: noun. One of a group of nymphs who lured mariners to their death on the rocks.
sis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siuslaw.
sister: noun. A daughter of the same parents as another person; a female sibling.
sit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sino-Tibetan languages.
site: noun. The place where anything is fixed; situation; local position
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.

stein: noun. A beer mug, usually made of ceramic or glass.
stern: adjective. Having a hardness and severity of nature or manner.
